content.full_text:
December 11, 1978

Honorable Sidney W. Kaufman  
Los Angeles Superior Court, Dept. 14  
111 North Hill Street  
Los Angeles, California 90012

Dear Judge Kaufman:

Pursuant to your request, this letter is to confirm, in part, my advice to you on Thursday, December 7, 1978.

You stated that during the month of November, you sold some unimproved real property and asked how you were to properly report this transaction and the promissory note that you now hold on that property on your Statement of Economic Interest, Form 721.

I advised that the property sold would be listed on Schedule 721-B, Interests in Real Property, where you would also report the date you sold the property. After a brief discussion, you mentioned that you would cross reference the promissory note on Schedule 721-A as an Investment at my suggestion. After checking with Mr. Ted Prim of our Conflicts Division in Sacramento, I find that I am in error on this portion of my advice. The promissory note is to be reported on Schedule B, Interests in Real Property. The income you received from the promissory note will be reported on Schedule 721-D, Income as required by Government Code Section 87207.

I hope that my error has not caused you any inconvenience; please accept my sincerest apologies. Should you have any questions concerning this matter, you may contact me at (213) 620-5196.

Sincerely,

Helen P. Andino  
Special Compliance Representative
